nextAction.expectedArtifact: structured receipt with scope labels: permission-state, denied-state, not-determined-state, simulator-permission-reset
nextAction.failureMeaning: Notification permission work remains generic validation rather than a permission workflow contract.
nextAction.successCondition: ShipGuard verify can distinguish local permission workflow proof from manual device proof.

proofBoundaries.localAutomation: Structured validation receipts can prove state handling and regression coverage.
proofBoundaries.physicalDevice: Physical-device prompt timing, OS-level permission UI, and release claims need manual/device proof.
proofBoundaries.simulator: Simulator proof can show reset/denied-state recovery, but not final physical-device prompt truth.

reportQualityQuestions: Did prepare identify notification/permission owner scopes, review-only lifecycle/plist surfaces, and forbidden entitlement/project changes?
Did verify require permission-state and denied-state proof instead of treating a generic test log as enough?
Did the verdict separate simulator denied-state proof from physical-device prompt proof before release claims?

authorized.reason: Synthetic source owner for notification permission copy and copy-only UI.
Synthetic test owner for permission-state validation.

forbiddenUnlessExplicit.reason: Entitlement changes are release/signing sensitive and require explicit authorization.
Project graph changes are forbidden unless the task explicitly authorizes Xcode project edits.

reviewOnly.reason: Permission purpose strings can change review behavior and require human review.
Notification registration lifecycle changes must be reviewed before editing.
Scene lifecycle notification routing must be reviewed before editing.

validationReceiptRequirements.expectedArtifact: structured validation receipt with permission-state scope labels
validationReceiptRequirements.failureMeaning: Permission-state behavior remains a generic test claim, not a permission workflow proof.
validationReceiptRequirements.successCondition: Tests or UI automation cover not-determined, denied, and authorized/provisional states.

validationReceiptRequirements.expectedArtifact: simulator screenshot, UI log, or receipt after resetting notification permission state
validationReceiptRequirements.failureMeaning: The denied-state recovery path remains unproven in a runnable local environment.
validationReceiptRequirements.successCondition: Denied-state recovery is observed after a simulator permission reset.

validationReceiptRequirements.expectedArtifact: physical-device prompt receipt or manually reviewed screenshot reference
validationReceiptRequirements.failureMeaning: ShipGuard must not treat simulator/source evidence as physical-device prompt proof.
validationReceiptRequirements.successCondition: Real-device prompt timing and wording are observed before release claims.

expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the actionability question in JSON and Markdown
report-quality emits a fixtureCandidates entry for the public synthetic case
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
the fixture exposes quickstartReplay so a fresh maintainer can reach or replay the first useful verdict
the fixture Markdown exposes Quickstart Replay without requiring JSON inspection
prepare fixtures connect goal, risk, scope, proof, claims, verdict, and next action through the replay contract
verify fixtures expose replay command, fast verdict, review packet, and next action
the fixture exposes domainRiskPack.validationReceiptRequirements for permission-state, denied-state, and not-determined-state scope labels
the fixture exposes a failure meaning that generic validation is not notification permission workflow proof
the fixture nextAction asks for structured receipt scope labels instead of a generic test log
the fixture Markdown renders receipt requirements, failure meanings, permission-state, denied-state, and the generic-test boundary
the fixture exposes simulator-denied-state-recovery and physical-device-prompt-boundary receipt requirements separately
the fixture exposes proofBoundaries explaining simulator proof is not physical-device prompt proof
the fixture nextAction distinguishes local simulator permission reset proof from remaining manual/device release proof
the fixture Markdown renders simulator-denied-state-recovery, simulator-permission-reset, physical-device-prompt-boundary, and the release-claim boundary
fixtureCandidate.privateDataPolicy: This materialized starter is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly remain explicit.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.
Register the fixture in validation only after the report-quality check passes.

fixtureCandidate.publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard report pair that reproduces this report-quality question without private app code, local paths, screenshots, customer data, app names, or proprietary source snippets.
fixtureCandidate.sourceQuestion: Did the verdict separate simulator denied-state proof from physical-device prompt proof before release claims?

quickstartReplay.boundary: Synthetic replay contract only; it does not authorize target-app work.
quickstartReplay.successSignal: shipguard-verdict.json returns pass, review, blocked, or incomplete with one nextAction.

reportQualityQuestions: Did the verdict separate simulator denied-state proof from physical-device prompt proof before release claims?

scanScope.note: Synthetic fixture; no private source tree was scanned.

scopeBoundary.purpose: Synthetic public fixture for ShipGuard report-quality behavior.
